subject: Re: Training a new model
body:
Hi,

you can try using this blank model 
<http://boxnet.warpem.com/models/BoxNet2_blank.zip>, but I predict the 
results won't be satisfactory. When training from scratch, a lot more 
iterations are needed than the number used in Warp for retraining. There is 
currently no way to perform training from scratch inside Warp.

May I ask how your particles differ from those already known to BoxNet? It 
would be very valuable to add such data to the central repository 
<http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=72>, and to include them when training 
from scratch for future releases.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Wednesday, March 6, 2019 at 4:37:09 PM UTC+1, A.Masoumzadeh wrote:
>
> Could you please tell me if I can train a new model from scratch?
> (Instead of re-training one of the available models)
>
>
